DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS JEFFERSON CHEN, KEVIN LEE, AND VALERIE LEE'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF GORDON MILLER TO SUBMIT TO A NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL EXAMINATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF GORDON MILLER TO SUBMIT TO SUBMIT TO A NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL EXAMINATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN GOOD CAUSE FOR THE EXAM. HOWEVER, THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO MEET AND CONFER TO ADDRESS WAYS TO MINIMIZE THE EXPENSE, BURDEN AND INCONVENIENCE OF THE EXAM ON PLAINTIFF GORDON MILLER AND HIS FAMILY, WHILE STILL ENABLING DEFENDANTS TO OBTAIN A FULL NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL EXAMINATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

Mtn For Order Compelling Pltf Gordon Miller To Submit To A Neuropsychology Examination
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS JEFFERSON CHEN, KEVIN LEE, AND VALERIE LEE'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER COMPELLING PLAINTIFF GORDON MILLER TO SUBMIT TO A NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL EXAMINATION.

APPEARANCES: Adrian Hern, Esq. of Goldstein, Gellman, Melbostad, Harris & McSparran LLP 673-5600 for pltfs & opposing parties; John C. Won, Esq. of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP 510/763-2324 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants' motion to compel plaintiff Gordon Miller to submit to submit to a neuropsychological examination is granted. Defendants have shown good cause for the exam. However, the parties are directed to meet and confer to address ways to minimize the expense, burden and inconvenience of the exam on plaintiff Gordon Miller and his family, while still enabling defendants to obtain a full neuropsychological examination. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported
